raw_response: {"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"CAS shall provide a configuration mechanism to define the set of quality checking methods available for application to environmental data."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"CAS shall maintain a persistent record of which quality checking method or methods were applied to each quality checking determination."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"CAS shall associate each recorded quality checking method with the specific environmental data or observation it was applied to."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"CAS shall store the identity or version of each quality checking method applied, in order to support traceability of quality checking determinations."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"CAS shall make the recorded quality checking method information retrievable for administrative review."},{"allocation":"CAS","requirement":"CAS shall prevent modification of recorded quality checking method information by unauthorized users."}]}

low_level_requirements.requirement: CAS shall provide a configuration mechanism to define the set of quality checking methods available for application to environmental data.
CAS shall maintain a persistent record of which quality checking method or methods were applied to each quality checking determination.
CAS shall associate each recorded quality checking method with the specific environmental data or observation it was applied to.
CAS shall store the identity or version of each quality checking method applied, in order to support traceability of quality checking determinations.
CAS shall make the recorded quality checking method information retrievable for administrative review.
CAS shall prevent modification of recorded quality checking method information by unauthorized users.

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Collector Services subsystem shall provide an interface to accept environmental data submitted from emergency vehicles.
The Collector Services subsystem shall validate that incoming data from emergency vehicles conforms to the Clarus standard data format prior to acceptance.
The Collector Services subsystem shall timestamp environmental data received from emergency vehicles upon acceptance.
The Collector Services subsystem shall record the source identifier of the emergency vehicle associated with each accepted environmental data submission.
The Collector Services subsystem shall forward accepted environmental data from emergency vehicles to downstream Clarus processing components.
The Collector Services subsystem shall reject and log environmental data submissions from emergency vehicles that do not conform to the accepted data format.

low_level_requirements.requirement: QChS shall complete the automated quality checking of newly received environmental data within a time budget that allows the Clarus system to publish that data within twenty minutes of data receipt.
QChS shall forward quality-checked environmental data to downstream publication processing immediately upon completion of quality checking to support the twenty-minute publication requirement.
QChS shall flag environmental data that has not completed quality checking within its allocated time budget so that it does not delay publication beyond twenty minutes of data receipt.
